steps.title: Welcome to the Lab
steps.instruction:
Welcome to the Token Optimization - The Credit Crunch lab.

In this hands-on sequence you will optimize a real workflow end to end. You will define a dependency upgrade and migration assistant workflow, author a lean instructions file for it, then use /create-skill and /create-agent to scaffold the supporting artifacts. Finally, you will inspect what context Copilot actually sends so you can optimize with evidence instead of guesswork.

The theme throughout: define the workflow first, then build artifacts that keep it token-efficient.

steps.title: Open a Scratch File
steps.instruction: We've opened a new scratch file so you can draft a workflow definition without touching your project files.

steps.title: Define the Migration Workflow
steps.instruction:
Write a short workflow specification for a dependency upgrade and migration assistant. Capture the four parts from the slide:
• Inputs: target package and version, the lockfile and manifest, the test command
• Steps: read release notes, apply documented codemods, update affected modules
• Guardrails: stay within the affected modules only
• Definition of done: the build passes, tests pass, and a concise summary lists the breaking changes addressed

Keep it tight. A clear definition is what stops an agent from over-exploring and burning tokens.
steps.tip: The guardrail "stay within affected modules only" is a cost control as much as a correctness rule. It keeps the agent from reading the whole repository.

steps.title: Panels Closed
steps.instruction:
We've closed the editor tab for you.

For future reference:
• New File: Ctrl+N

steps.title: Open a Scratch File
steps.instruction: We've opened a fresh scratch file so you can draft a lean instructions file for the migration workflow.

steps.title: Draft Lean Standing Rules
steps.instruction:
Write only the standing rules that should apply to every migration interaction, for example:

// Use the project's existing package manager.
// Run the test suite with the documented test command.
// Touch only the modules affected by the upgrade.
// Summarize breaking changes addressed at the end.

Notice what you are not putting here: the detailed upgrade procedure. That belongs in a skill, loaded on demand. Instructions load on every request in scope, so keep them lean.
steps.tip: A bloated instructions file is a recurring tax on every prompt. If a section is only sometimes relevant, it belongs in a skill, not in instructions.

steps.title: Panels Closed
steps.instruction:
We've closed the editor tab for you.

For future reference:
• New File: Ctrl+N

steps.title: Open Copilot Chat
steps.instruction: We've opened Copilot Chat so you can scaffold a skill for the detailed migration procedure.

steps.title: Scaffold a Skill with /create-skill
steps.instruction:
In the chat input, run the skill scaffolding command and describe the procedure from your workflow definition:

/create-skill A dependency upgrade procedure: read release notes and breaking changes, apply documented codemods, update only the affected modules, then run the test suite and summarize the breaking changes addressed.

The detailed procedure belongs in a skill so it only loads when the task matches, instead of being re-explained in every chat.
steps.tip: Reach for /create-skill when a procedure is repeatable and worth reusing across the team. A precise description is what makes the skill trigger on the right tasks.

steps.title: Panels Closed
steps.instruction:
We've closed the sidebar for you.

For future reference:
• Copilot Chat: Ctrl+Shift+I

steps.title: Open Copilot Chat
steps.instruction: We've opened Copilot Chat so you can scaffold an agent persona for the migration workflow.

steps.title: Scaffold a Persona with /create-agent
steps.instruction:
In the chat input, run the agent scaffolding command and describe a focused role with a constrained tool set:

/create-agent A migration assistant that upgrades dependencies. It reads release notes, edits only the affected modules, and runs the test suite. Keep its tools limited to file edits and running tests so it stays on task.

A constrained tool set is a cost lever: an agent with fewer tools explores less and spends fewer tokens.
steps.tip: Reach for /create-agent when a workflow recurs and benefits from a dedicated operator with a fixed role. Scope the tools tightly to the workflow's needs.

steps.title: Panels Closed
steps.instruction:
We've closed the sidebar for you.

For future reference:
• Copilot Chat: Ctrl+Shift+I

steps.title: Open Copilot Chat
steps.instruction: We've opened Copilot Chat so you can send a request and then inspect what it actually cost.

steps.title: Read the Debug Metadata
steps.instruction:
Send a small request, then open Copilot's debug metadata for that turn and read the cost fields:
• usage: compare prompt_tokens to completion_tokens and note how much of the input was cached_tokens
• tools (n): every available tool schema is part of the request
• copilotUsage: the real AIC spend for that single turn

This turns token optimization into evidence: bloated instruction files, oversized context, and large tool sets all show up here.
steps.tip: Menu paths evolve with the Copilot tooling. If you don't see the debug metadata, check the Command Palette for a Copilot debug or log-level command and confirm the steps against your VS Code version.

steps.title: Panels Closed
steps.instruction:
We've closed the sidebar for you.

For future reference:
• Copilot Chat: Ctrl+Shift+I

steps.title: Lab Complete!
steps.instruction:
Congratulations! You have completed the Token Optimization lab.

You practiced the full workflow-first loop:
• Defined a dependency migration workflow with inputs, steps, guardrails, and a definition of done
• Authored a lean instructions file for the standing rules
• Used /create-skill to scaffold the detailed procedure as an on-demand skill
• Used /create-agent to scaffold a constrained migration assistant persona
• Inspected the assembled request so you can optimize tokens with evidence

Define the workflow first, capture it in durable artifacts, and let every credit buy more value.
